name: Frontier Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon ground dried pasilla chili peppers
6 boned and skinned chicken breast halves
1 large ripe banana, peeled and sliced
2 tablespoons jalapeno pepper, seeded and minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl.
2. Sprinkle each side of each chicken breast with half teaspoon of the seasoning mix and rub it in well with your hands.
3. Heat the oil in a large skillet or 5-quart pot over high heat until the oil begins to smoke, about 4 minutes.
4. Brown the seasoned breasts (in batches if necessary) for 2 to 3 minutes per side, then remove them from the pan. The oil and turmeric give the chicken a yellow-gold color that is really dramatic.
5. To the same skillet (or pot), add 1 cup of onions, 3/8 cup of each color of bell pepper, the banana, and remaining seasoning mix. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes. If necessary to prevent burning, add 1/4 cup stock and deglaze the pot by scraping to loosen any brown bits; you may have to lower the heat to medium.
6. Add garlic, ginger, and flour and stir until the flour is thoroughly absorbed.
7. Add remaining bell peppers and onions, cilantro, and jalapeno peppers. Cook for 5 minutes.
8. Add tomatoes and remaining stock and return the chicken and accumulated juices to the pan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer until the chicken is done, about 10 minutes.
9. Serve hot.
